body_txt: Certain facts or events are presented in a series that conflicts with related information given in earlier episodes. Usually not as overt as a Retcon , as it often occurs from not double-checking the Universe Bible or fan-made encyclopedias. Usually as simple as one episode stating a character was 30 years old and another episode mentioning the year they were born and it's off by 5 years . When you get to Negative Continuity , this becomes more the rule than the exception. Compare Continuity Snarl and Continuity Drift , which are for multiple works contradicting each other rather than a single work contradicting itself. A common feature of Long-Runners , as it's difficult to keep track of continuity after multiple years or decades, especially as new writers are brought on board. Also ubiquitous with prequels , as trying to tell a story that fits perfectly within "later" canon can be near impossible. Broad Strokes is often used to paper over the inconsistencies, trying to make sense of a lot of contradictory information into a coherent whole. See also Characterization Marches On and Early-Installment Weirdness , which is often rife with these kind of continuity issues. Contrast Revision , which is when a retcon fits within the broad spirit of the original information and may give a reason it is different, such as Unreliable Narrator .
